transcript.clean_text: the weather did not look promising on the 134 kilometer run to valdepenas stage 11 of this year's welter the shortest of the race well after a 31 rider breakaway went up the road on stage 10 how many would be brave enough to take on more rolling terrain with a category 2 test followed by a nasty ramp up to the finish line itself with inclines of around 20 and more in the end five riders were brave enough blanket from alpacin bow from oscar tell laster from cairo roral van hook from lots of saddam magnus court whit nielson stage winner from stage six well the nerves were on display it was a tightly packed peloton when the climbing began minute 53 was the gap when a touch of wheels brought down many embarrassment more than anything else but a reminder of how fickle this race could be just ask primos roglic of course who crashed the day prior old chrissy nike wearing the leader's jersey and the duty of protecting it falling on into marsha wanti but roglich never that far behind so many teams had options on the day as a result there were many who were prepared to keep their breakaway on a shortish leash and when we came to the penultimate climb and the run to it it was bound to detonate but the man pulling the pin favorite amongst them had to be magnus caught nielsen away he went and with 16 kilometers to go he was aiming for the last peak before the descent that would take us up to the wall if he'd held a margin of around three quarters of a minute by the time he reached that seven and a half eight kilometers to go he may have had a chance bike exchange wanted to get michael matthews in the frame movistar were doing a lot of work as well thinking about good positions for enric mass and miguel anger lopez first roller the dice from a depleted peloton came from david de la cruz from uae team emirates he's starting the day with quite a deficit running in 13th place and wanted to find some time well time was running out not only for him and this push but for those up ahead as well caruso just stealing some mountains points on that category 2 test to increase his lead in the king of the mountains competition but with 4.1 kilometers to go is a half minute gap could magnus caught nielsen make it eager manal was brought to the front it was strong riding yet again from his teammate dylan van baal 15 seconds then heading for the flam rouge and magnus court knew that the ramps were up ahead this desperate wall had been raced three times before a huge challenge roglich wanted to go for it and called on cepcus to offer him a wheel and then mass came round and went on the attack magnus caught up ahead and it was to be mass versus roglic ultimately with jack hague also playing a role past magnus court they sailed within sight of the line and the final test was to prove to be a huge one some gaps were opened up but in the end it was roglich that gapped the entire field driving away from mass to give himself a stage win another in this race and indeed a gap enhanced of course by the bonuses average pace for the day 42 kilometers per hour on rolling terrain in the heat around 31 celsius it was tough roglich took the stage ahead of mass megalangle lopez making it two movie star riders on the day's podium and the man weaving at the back there in the pink magnus caught nielsen his dreams shattered at the very last within sight of the line surely a combativity award for him but surely he would have hoped for more roglic congratulated by mass it looks like this is a battle between those two and megalanga lopez they the one two three on this punishing test they're now finishing ninth at 11 seconds an old christian eike maintaining the lead overall but for how long roglic with his second stage win looks very strong heading for the high ground which will be at the weekend iking will stay in the red jersey tomorrow and in fact the gap between himself and his nearest rival for the time being guillermo stays at 58 seconds they followed each other home today but then roglich massen lopez and hague with banal yates cuss and grosshartner rounding out your top 10 overall another day in the red jersey intermarche wanti thinking all their christmases have come at once and so to stage 12 the run to cordoba another day with a long reach to a lumpy finale 19.2 this time too will it be one for the breakaway it's a downhill run to the line it kind of begs the question every single day what is going to happen the answer of course always is we just don't know it's what makes la velta such a special race you
